violations.text: The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink, as the test strips on site expired 12/1/2025. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2026

Working containers (spray bottles) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. The 3 bay sink will be used to sanitize equipment and utensils until the dishwasher is serviced or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2025

(Chemical) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Milk and cheese observed held at 43-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Milk and cheese were placed in freezer to rapidly chill. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The milk cooler was not in good repair, not holding food at 41 degrees F or below. All food requiring refrigeration was relocated, and the unit is not to be used until it is functioning properly. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the milk cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. PIC informed to sanitize utensils and equipment in 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024

The establishment thermometer observed not reading properly. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/19/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the milk cooler inside have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024
